name: Diana - Practicing Safe Hex - Now Even Hexier (Hard Mode)
description_md: _-_ ***This is the second major version of a Diana deck that i have been playing A LOT recently. Versions of it (including this one) have cleared hard mode TCU twice, and half of hard mode carcosa paired with differnt Joe and Minh decks. And a variant closer to [the last published version](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/14103/diana-stanley-practicing-safe-hex-hard-mode-1.0) has cleared labyrinths of lunacy, and both parts of guardians of the abyse (standard mode) ending each with negligible damage or horror.*** ***This version was derived from some of [the comments on the last one](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/14103/diana-stanley-practicing-safe-hex-hard-mode-1.0). Huge shout out to @Yann for his suggestion of making a pure caster version. You can find [his wonderful discussion article here](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/14125/diana-stanley-the-lucky-bastard-hard-building-guide-6.0).*** *The result, a bit like the last iteration is still on the complex end. This is definitely not one for people who are new to the game. However it is hopefully one for which the write up will be more intuitive as the last one relied on a lot more token manipulation.* *My apologies again for the length of the write up on this one. Its just such a complex deck there's a lot more detail needed. I will try to thin it down in edits when i find time, but im not sure how soon that will be. Hopefully whatever i do next will be more straightforward.* *Finally, part of the extra length is due to the addition of a few extra details that ive not previously used, mainly the slots list, the cost table and a few similar bullet point list overviews at the start of some of sections highlighting some details like which spell to prioritise. If you found these useful or cumbersome (and please be honest) i`d like to know so i can include/exclude them on future write ups. I realise the draw and list is unlikely to be needed in another ID of course. But im curious to hear about the others.* _-_ ### ***OUT OF ORDER - (Overview/TL:DR)*** * **1st Hand** ....... [Twilight Blade](/card/05013) * **2nd Hand** ...... [Ritual Candles](/card/02029) * **Accessory** ..... [Holy Rosary](/card/01059) / [Police Badge](/card/01027) * **Body** .............. *Unused* * **1st Arcane** .... Clue Spell [Sixth Sense](/card/05322) > [Rite of Seeking](/card/02028) if you took it. * **2nd Arcane** ... Combat Spell ([Shriv](/card/01060) > [Mists](/card/04029)/[Shards](/card/04310) > [Wither](/card/05321)) * **Tarot** .............. *Unused* * **Ally** ................. Early: [Arcane Initiate](/card/01063), Late: [Olive McBride](/card/04197) *A fast off the starting block, mixed clues and combat, thematic Diana build (she even holds a [dagger](/card/05013) and a [Candle](/card/02029)!). The deck is focuses around card draw and rapid escalation to both find the appropriate spell and boost its base skill really high really early in the game making a highly consistent all rounder mystic thats tough as nails to boot. We basically boost to 6 or 7 super fast, then convert all our actions through via spells (which also add 2), and use cancels to overcome the drawbacks on the spells. With 8 to 10 base skill on spell based tests, [Olive McBride](/card/04197) can fish out (and the odd ) for things like [Sixth Sense (4)](/card/05322) despite needing to resolve 2 tokens, while providing 3 soak for [big Shriv](/card/02306).* *The deck has 12 highly flexible -able cards. Almost all of which cost 0 or 1 to play. This rises to 14 [after your first xp](/card/05315) and there are 4 [less flexible](/card/04197) ones [too](/card/02190). With your these are cost neutral or cash generating. Combined with [Rosary](/card/01059)s and [Arcane Initiates](/card/01063), close to* ***half of the deck draws*** *a new card and close to* ***half of the deck boosts ,*** all at minimal cost and ***almost all as fast actions.*** *Setting up spells much faster than most mystics.* ***Play as many or + cards you can*** *in rounds 1 and 2 (its once per phase not round) and just spam them* ***irrespective of what you are cancelling until you have 3 or 4 ,*** *yes even [Ward](/card/01065). If you had a fast card that simply said gain 1 penny, draw 1 card and gain +1 you would play it on sight. Cancelling something is a bonus. Xp adds +2 to most spell assets, getting us to 5 or 6 effective from just 2 or 3 /[Rosary](/card/01059)s and is very achievable on turn 1 or 2. First scenario this might be a turn or so later so we bridge that brief gap with skill pips which can be used on her X333 statline as one shots later on.* [Seal of the Elder Sign](/card/03312), [Terrible Secret](/card/05015), and [Twilight Blade](/card/05013) are used to keep space under your ID and keep the train rolling. Even though [Prepared for the Worst](/card/02184) isnt a perfect tutor, you are milling your deck hard so 9 cards can be half the deck fairly easilly. Its not ideal but its good enough given how important [The Blade](/card/05013) is. Once you have it, you effectively have a 13 card hand and can replay your cancels making you an absolute brick. Between all the cancels, super high , and a TON of soak, you wont care about horror. This lets you use [Arcane Research](/card/04109) to get + spells early, and use [Shriv (5)](/card/02306) like you have always wanted to. Want to be cocky? Cancel the token horror with ["I've had worse…" (2)](/card/05315) and gain 3 pennies and a card for your trouble! With this much you can even use [Olive McBride](/card/04197) to sideline the guaranteeing a 3 damage hit. _-_ ### ***ON WARD AND UP WARD - (Upgrade Path)*** #### *PRIORITIES:* 1. **[Uncage the Soul](/card/03033) ["I've had worse…" (2)](/card/05315)** *- The MVP (see XXX Section)* THEN GET SOME + SPELLS IN: * **[Sixth Sense](/card/05158) [Sixth Sense (4)](/card/05322)** * **[Shrivelling](/card/01060) [Shrivelling (3)](/card/02154)** * **[Mists of R'lyeh](/card/04029) [Shards of the Void](/card/04310) or [Mists of R'lyeh (4)](/card/04271)**. * **[Wither](/card/05157) [Wither (4)](/card/05321)** * **[Shrivelling (3)](/card/02154) [Shrivelling (5)](/card/02306)** - Technically taken for the bonus damage - but still pretty important early Each +1 you can build into a spell, means 1 less card needs playing before you are up and running. If you have played 2 or 3 s (or [Rosary](/card/01059)/[Police Badge](/card/01027)) Your +2 spells bring you to 5 or 6. Playing 2 or 3 s around turn 2 is easy, playing 3 or 4 might happen around turn 3-5 ish as you use the easy ones first. Using spells earlier also opens up opportunities responding to them. More details on how to use these are in Section XXX for Cluing and Section XXX for Combat, including how to deal with horror from [Big Shriv](/card/02306), and how to use [Shards of the Void](/card/04310) without nerfing your bag. Pick these in whatever order matches your team composition. I usually take [Sixth Sense (4)](/card/05322) first since you usually need to clue earlier than fight. . #### *OTHER [ARCANE RESEARCH](/card/04109) TARGETS:* * **[Ward of Protection](/card/01065) [Ward of Protection (2)](/card/03270)** * **[Storm of Spirits](/card/03153) [Storm of Spirits (3)](/card/52008)** - *Adds and Damage.* * **[Deny Existence](/card/05032) [Deny Existence (5)](/card/05280)** Note that [Arcane Research](/card/04109) only works when going from a lower lvl spell to a higher one, not when adding new spells like [Seal of the Elder Sign](/card/03312). In an 8 part campaign, you can get 7 sets of 2xp from [Arcane Research](/card/04109). With the 7 or 8 from the priorities list, this make 14 possible options so you may as well spend normal xp on the best ones (ie the priority spells). You wont run out of targets. . #### *OTHER NORMAL XP TARGETS:* * **[Holy Rosary](/card/01059) [Police Badge](/card/01027)** *See note* * **[Take the Initiative](/card/04150) [Seal of the Elder Sign](/card/03312)** - *see XXX Section* * **[Defiance](/card/02190) [Counterspell](/card/04110)** - *see XXX Section* * **[Charisma](/card/02158)** *- both allies in play at once* * **[Arcane Initiate](/card/01063) [Arcane Initiate](/card/03271)** - *Untested. But no doom requirement looks nice on paper here, Possibly take after [Charisma](/card/02158)* NOTE: [Police Badge](/card/01027) is not the most thematic choice, but its safe to skip if you dislike it. This is unfortunately one of the few action boosting options available to [Diana](/card/05004), which makes it one of the best movement tools she has and movement is something she lacks unless you flex in [Astral Travel](/card/03034). Use it for the early game then fire the action for a late game boost. Or once cash is plentiful late game, you can use to basically spend 3 to gain an action _-_ _-_ # *DETAILED PILOTING* _-_ _-_ ### ***INITIATION - (Mulligan)*** Keep (in order of priority) * **[Twilight Blade](/card/05013)**, *dont keep [Prepared for the Worst](/card/02184)* * **[Arcane Initiate](/card/01063)** * **[Sixth Sense](/card/05322)** *, or damage spell if needed* * **3x + cards**, *1x [Badge](/card/01027)/[Rosary](/card/01059) > ["IHW" (2)](/card/05315) > [Dark Proph](/card/04032) > [Ward](/card/01065) > [Counterspell](/card/04110) > [Deny](/card/05032) > [Delay](/card/05021)* Mulligan the rest in hope of finding the above. *Dont keep [Eldritch Inspiration](/card/05033), [Defiance](/card/02190) or [Olive McBride](/card/04197) for + unless you have a clever plan. They arnt as cheap/easy/reliable to trigger s from.* . _-_ ### ***WHAT DOESNT KILL HER MAKES HER STRONGER - (cash, , draw, and +)*** Sorry for the screen dump, but this is a complex deck and its worth being aware of its break down. . #### *TRUE COST TABLE:* *The cost curve generated by arkhamdb is a bit misleading with your , skill cards, cash generating cards, and several duplicated assets. Here is a very rough but hopefully more useful way of displaying it.* * <0: 11111 * =0: 111111111111111111 * =1: 111111 * =2: 111 * =3: 11111 * 4+: Pip fodder (1x of each of the following are included as =0 cost): 11111111 * [Shrivelling](/card/01060), [Sixth Sense](/card/05158), [Wither](/card/05157), [Holy Rosary](/card/01059), [Arcane Initiate](/card/01063), [Olive McBride](/card/04197), [Prepared for the Worst](/card/02184), [Ritual Candles](/card/02029). . #### *DRAW, PASSIVE + AND CARDS:* RELIABLE DRAW AND : * 2x [Arcane Initiate](/card/01063) (13 spells in deck, rises to 17 with xp) * 1x [Dark Insight](/card/05014) * 1x [Eldritch Inspiration](/card/05033) -*spell* * 2x [Dark Prophecy](/card/04032) * 2x [Delay the Inevitable](/card/05021) * 2x [Ward of Protection](/card/01065) -*spell* * 2x [Deny Existence](/card/05032) -*spell* PSEUDO DRAW AND : * 2x [Defiance](/card/02190) * 2x [Prepared for the Worst](/card/02184) * 2x [Olive McBride](/card/04197) STUFF YOU CAN ADD WITH XP: * 2x ["I've had worse…"](/card/05315) (Over [Uncage the Soul](/card/03033)) * 2x [Counterspell](/card/04110) (Over [Defiance](/card/02190)) -*spell* ADDITIONAL NON BASED BOOSTS * 2x [Holy Rosary](/card/01059) / [Police Badge](/card/01027) * 2x [Ritual Candles](/card/02029) (sort of) . Why does this matter? Well, mainly because it made me happy when i realised it was possible and I spent ages mapping it all out so im dying to show ppl, but also it means you are never short of + boosters or draw cards. Why? because about half of the time a draw card will feed you another draw card, and when you add the 1 a turn from upkeep you get a happy little chain ... most turns ... basically look at the pretty numbers! ...ooOOOooo! **EARLY CAMPAIGN DECK COMPOSITION METRICS:** * *TOTAL DRAW ....................... 12 (+ 6 pseudo) ~1/3 of deck * * *TOTAL ............................ 10 (+ 4 Pseudo) ~1/4 of deck * * *TOTAL + BOOSTS ...... 14 (+4 pseudo) ~1/3 of deck * **POTENTIAL LATER CAMPAIGN DECK COMPOSITION METRICS:** * *TOTAL DRAW ....................... 18 (+ 4 pseudo) ~1/2 of deck * * *TOTAL ............................ 14 (+ 2 Pseudo) ~1/3 of deck * * *TOTAL + BOOSTS ...... 18 (+ 4 pseudo) ~1/2 of deck * . #### *GETTING HIGH FAST* With all that i mind, how do we get to this magical 3 or 4 (equivalent to 5 or 6 with a +2 spell) that you keep going on about asap? (See Upgrading section). * **Play as many + and as you can at the start irrespective of what you are cancelling** I cannot stress this enough. You are not playing these to cancel things at this point in the game, they are your cash, draw and + engine. You can play 1 card per phase (not round) and [Rosary](/card/01059) and [Ritual Candles](/card/02029) dont count. Plan out what card you can play each phase, these are your options: * MYTHOS PHASE: *Anything*: [Dark Insight](/card/05014). *Treachery Only*: [Ward of Protection](/card/01065). *Damage Taken*:[Deny Existence](/card/05032), ["I've had worse…"](/card/05315), (circumstantial: [Delay the Inevitable](/card/05021)). *Test Taken:* [Dark Prophecy](/card/04032), [Olive McBride](/card/04197), (circumstantial: [Counterspell](/card/04110)/[Defiance](/card/02190)). * INVESTIGATOR PHASE: *Assets*: [Badge](/card/01027)/[Rosary](/card/01059), [Candles](/card/02029). *Tests*: [Dark Prophecy](/card/04032), [Olive McBride](/card/04197), (circumstantial: [Defiance](/card/02190), [Counterspell](/card/04110), [Eldritch Inspiration](/card/05033) - [Olive McBride](/card/04197) can help). *Damage/Horror/Enemies*: [Dodge](/card/01023), [Deny Existence](/card/05032), ["I've had worse…"](/card/05315) *Played [Drawn to the Flame](/card/01064)?:* [Ward](/card/01065), [Dark Insight](/card/05014), plus any of the damage/skill ones. * ENEMY PHASE: *Enemy Attacks* ["I've had worse…"](/card/05315), [Deny Existence](/card/05032), [Delay the Inevitable](/card/05021). * UPKEEP PHASE: *Weakness Drawn:* [Dark Insight](/card/05014), its rare but it happens and if you happen to be at other players locations your odds increase a little too. If its [Terrible Secret](/card/05015) you can use [Deny Existence](/card/05032) instead. * **This basically means you always cancel the first encounter card** There is probably a reason you get [Dark Insight](/card/05014) in your starting hand. It looks to me like its in the design that she is intended to cancel something early. If possible use another card since [Dark Insight](/card/05014) is very versatile, but fire that [Ward](/card/01065) off without regret. * **Self harming or taking actions to trigger a card is fine while low on .** Think of it as taking an action to draw 1 card gain +1, and refund a penny. If you are happy to play [Rosary](/card/01059) for an action, you're happy to do this. * **Haunted effects often deal damage Use these.** Test raw using your 3 if needed. If you pass get a clue which is fine. If you fail fire a card. . #### MORE TRICKS * **You usually get the boost from your during the test you used the card.** Check the timing charts, you check your skill vs test strength at Step 6, but you reveal or resolve token effects at Steps 3 & 4. Which means you use your before you check if you have passed. This works for: [Dark Prophecy](/card/04032)/[Olive McBride](/card/04197)/[Counterspell](/card/04110) (token cancel is at Step 3), [Eldritch Inspiration](/card/05033) (Spell token effects happen at step 4), and ["I've had worse…"](/card/05315)/[Delay the Inevitable](/card/05021) (on horror/damage from [Shrivelling](/card/01060)/[Storm of Spirits](/card/03153) also at Step 4)). * **This means the right cancel in hand is like having a [Ritual Candles](/card/02029) down.** You can act as if the appropriate token your cancel triggers off had a 1 point lower modifier. If you dont draw it, you dont play it. If you do draw it, play the card, adjust your and get a +1 before you check skill. Its like having a baby [Lucky!](/card/01080)* * **When using [Olive McBride](/card/04197), you can pick which cancel to use after seeing the tokens**. Pretty much all of the relevant cancels run off tokens. [Olive McBride](/card/04197) works off cancelling any token but you decide whether to use her or the normal cancels after seeing the draw. So, in a pinch if you happen to draw 3 high modifier non tokens, you can use your on [Olive McBride](/card/04197) herself even if you couldnt use a cancel which might be enough to clinch a pass. Think of her as a back up. * **Use [Olive McBride](/card/04197) to fish for [Defiance](/card/02190) tokens** Its not perfect, but it helps you trigger a on [Defiance](/card/02190) more easily (you only get the if you DO ignore the token effect, ie if you drew the right one. Nulifying the modifier when you do so means you also only face 1 token picked from 2 worth of modifier if you get one. * **Its fine to cancel a positive effect with [Eldritch Inspiration](/card/05033)**. If you draw a with [Sixth Sense](/card/05158) or [Wither](/card/05157), and you have the choice between using [Eldritch Inspiration](/card/05033) and the spell`s internal ability. A lot of the time you may be unable to benefit from the ability, or you might just think the penny, card and +1 is better, especially with the lvl0 versions of the spells - [Wither (0)](/card/05157) is almost identical test impact wise and similarly [Sixth Sense](/card/05158) might only be using the token to get a better shroud. Baring in mind you are trying to use spells for most actions. You will get this option more often than it looks on paper. * **you have 6 skill cards in the deck if you include [Uncage the Soul](/card/03033)'s pips** This helps you deal with early game checks while building up, and is especially important for sc1 before you get +2 spells in. _-_ ### ***IGNORANCE IS BLISS - Keeping the Train Rolling*** Once you hit 5 cards under the ID, youre draw engine will briefly stall. This will happen pretty much every game. Its fine especially if you have the spell you need down. But once you hit this you sort of want to keep clearing a little space to get the cash and draw flowing again. * **Get your [Blade](/card/05013) down ASAP**. This is the only deck ive ever put a [Prepared for the Worst](/card/02184) in that has less than 3 weapons. You deck usually jams up when you have about 20 cards left in the deck. At this point youve had about a 1 in 3 chance of seeing the blade by chance (its slightly better due to the mulligan). Each [Prepared for the Worst](/card/02184) gives you about another 50/50 shot. It also shuffles your deck if the blade was going to be more than 9 cards later. Its not perfect. But its still worth it given the benefits of the blade. Reusing those cancels (especially ["I've had worse…"](/card/02261) and [Dark Prophecy](/card/04032) (with [Sixth Sense (4)](/card/05322) is a huge bonus too. * **Get [Seal of the Elder Sign](/card/03312) when you have the chance**. Straightforward enough. Pretty much guarentees a test pass and lets you take a card of your choosing from under the ID leaving space for you to it again immediately. Tides you over while looking for the blade. * **Use [Olive McBride](/card/04197)** The more you use her the more often you will see s. You will be using her once youre is high anyway for bonus clues (See section XXX). This is just a bonus of that method. * **Use [Terrible Secret](/card/05015)** It lets you cull a card or two of your choice. Use it to make space equal to the number of cards in your hand you recon you can soon. Unless you have your [Blade](/card/05013) down and perhaps want to keep something special back. _-_ ### ***YOUR NOT SO [TERRIBLE SECRET](/card/05015) - (Dealing with and benefiting from it)*** You are deliberately drawing hard. You are going to see this. It really isnt that bad. * **You WANT to cull cards from under your deck a bit** See Section on keeping space open (just above) - This actually helps you. * **You have a ton of soak, its not direct horror** [Olive McBride](/card/04197) has 3 san, [Arcane Initiate](/card/01063) has 2 as does [Holy Rosary](/card/01059). You can put the horror on these. * **You can chose to do a little of each** You usually want to cull a card or two to make space (see above), and remember that you only needed 2 or 3 cards under your ID at the start of the game for your +2 spells to be working at 5 or 6 base. So you only need to take 2 or 3 horror. You can pick the amount of horror to take. You can also let some of this through with the plan to heal it back later via [Deny Existence (5)](/card/05280), or just safe in the knowlegde you have that many cancels in the deck and such high potential for encounter tests that you wont take that much horror later. * **Your Achillies Heal!** [Deny Existence](/card/05032) can be used to "ignore" the horror from [Terrible Secret](/card/05015). It is applied as a single lump blow just like ([Rotting Remains](/card/01163) does, so a single card can block it. And it can be "ignored" even if it says it cant be cancelled (see the FAQ on the [Terrible Secret](/card/05015) card entry). This hilariously means [Deny Existence (5)](/card/05280) can be used to not only block the weakness, but actually heal you for up to 5 san. This is sometimes worth breaking the "fire all irrespective of use" rule if you dont have the soaks in play. Though bear in mind if you have the [Blade](/card/05013) down, you can still use a spent [Deny Existence](/card/05032) from under the ID, and that [Twilight Blade](/card/05013) will appear before [Terrible Secret](/card/05015) in most games if you are using [Prepared for the Worst](/card/02184). Similarly [Arcane Initiate](/card/01063) can find the [Deny Existence](/card/05032)s. _-_ ### ***OLIVE YOU CLUE - (Clues!)*** * 2x [Sixth Sense](/card/05158) - PRIMARY * 2x [Drawn to the Flame](/card/01064) - PRIMARY * 2x [Take the Initiative](/card/04150) - can be 6 once. Upgrade to [Seal of the Elder Sign](/card/03312) and it can be an autoclue up to shroud 5. * 2x [Defiance](/card/02190) + 1 pip from a spare card (usually [Prepared for the Worst](/card/02184) after you have the blade) = an effective 6. If you want to be more cluey, swap [Mists of R'lyeh](/card/04029)/[Wither](/card/05157) for [Rite of Seeking](/card/02028)s, and potentially bring [St. Hubert's Key](/card/03269) over [Holy Rosary](/card/01059) (Needs you to add pips). ***CLUE TOOLS .................... 4 Major (Inc. 2 evergreen), 4 Minor: ~1/4 of deck*** This should be fairly obvious but there are some tips: * **[Sixth Senses](/card/05158) ability is similar (and stacks with) [Ritual Candles](/card/02029)**. Because you can potentially pick the shroud you want to use AFTER seeing a token. So if you have a valid target with a lower shroud where these tokens would pass on, you can act as if their modifiers were in range. Pair this with the notes on the timing of cards, and tokens become beneficial. * **Use [Olive McBride](/card/04197) and [Dark Prophecy](/card/04032) with [Sixth Sense (4)](/card/05322) (and [Wither (4)](/card/05321) see below).** Once youve got to 6 or 7 via s and [Rosarys](/card/01059), the +2 built into the spell gets you to an effective 8 or 9 . tokens in TCU at least tend to be around -3 and -4 ish, but with [Ritual Candles](/card/02029), even needing to resolve 2 tokens can turn 2 tokens into a total of -5 quite easilly, and/or snipe a convenient shroud. This goes a long way so long as you arnt targetting 2 high shroud locations at once. Without meaning to stating the obvious too much [Dark Prophecy](/card/04032) does a similar thing once can be reused via [Seal of the Elder Sign](/card/03312) and [Twilight Blade](/card/05013), but is fine either at lower setup or for 2 high shroud locations. It can even boost your mid test (see timing tricks section). * **[Drawn to the Flame](/card/01064) both gets you clues and fishes for things to from.** Its good early game for both growth and an early clue tool when digging for [Sixth Sense](/card/05158). Its good late game as both an auto clue and 2 clues for 1 action. Diana can handle encounter cards. _-_ ### ***WITCH SLAP - (Enemy Handling)*** Tools Available: * 2x [Shrivelling](/card/01060) - PRIMARY * 2x [Storm of Spirits](/card/03153) - PRIMARY * 1x [Mists of R'lyeh](/card/04029)/[Shards](/card/04310) - Best backup * 1x [Wither](/card/05157) * 1x [Twilight Blade](/card/05013) - Not amazing, but is at least accurate. * 2x [Take the Initiative](/card/04150) - can be used to evade or deal 1 damage. ***ENEMY MANAGEMENT... 5 Major, 4 Minor (inc. 2 evergreen): ~1/4 of deck*** Most of this will be obvious if you have read the above. But there are a few tips and tricks. * **You are a mixed clues and combat flexible player. You are not a combat powerhouse capable of carrying the team**. The biggest problems with the latter is that [Diana](/card/05004) doesnt have a huge amount of movement or action cards to get over to other players and most of her high damage options are spells with limited uses. You can however carry your own and pack a punch for a boss fight when needed. I know [Diana Stanley](/card/05004) was a fighter in another game, but please dont use this deck as a purist fighter, and if you do dont blame me. This is not that game and I didnt do it. *Can politely I ask that we dont turn the comments section into a series of questions about how to maker her a fighter. Thats not what this deck is doing and I would rather the comments were available for ppl to ask questions about the deck. /endgrump.* * **GET THE [BIG SHRIV](/card/01060)** You know youve wanted to do this since it came out. Diana is perfect for it. With +3 built in, it reaches 10 at max so it rarely misses. You are fine for horror hits. you have a TON of soak ([Olive](/card/04197), [Rosary](/card/01059), [Initiate](/card/01063)). You can also cancel it with [Delay the Inevitable](/card/05021), ["I've had worse…"](/card/05315), [Eldritch Inspiration](/card/05033), [Counterspell](/card/04110). Thats upto about 1/3rd of the deck that can be used to mitigate this. Notice that ["I've had worse…"](/card/05315) actually earns you 3 pennies, a card and +1 if you use it. Suddenly the [Olive McBride](/card/04197) trick for token fishing can be used to guarentee a hit (ie sideline the if you draw it) so long as you have a way of dealing with the effect and occasionally boost your econ engine! * **You can use [Delay the Inevitable](/card/05021) over and over again with [Twilight Blade](/card/05013)**. The blade only stops your while playing a card. Once [Delay the Inevitable](/card/05021) is in play you are no longer playing it. So when you spend it, you can put it back under your ID with your , get a penny refund and draw a card. You paid 2 to play it, got 1 back and you get 1 penny from upkeep. Or to put it another way, you can dodge once a turn indefinitely. ***SHOUT OUT TO @Cuherdir FOR POINTING THIS OUT*** * **You dont always need to put attack spells into play asap**. If you have a damage cancel in hand, you can wait until you draw an enemy, then block the AoO from putting the asset down before you use it, or just use [Storm of Spirits](/card/03153) from hand. This lets you wait until you have a better tool (ie wait to see [Shrivelling](/card/01060)) without too much risk. It also lets you progress the game rather than waste time building a board. Especially if you have unlimited [Delay the Inevitable](/card/05021)s. * **Using [Shards of the Void](/card/04310) without nerfing the bag** This means you can keep [Shards](/card/04310) back in hand until you need to attack with it. Once it is in play, spend the zero asap. You have never used the chaos bag with the zero taken out, and if you think you can land it, you can use [Olive](/card/04197) to fish for a zero for the bonus damage. If you get one, the +2 from a zero on the card kicks in before the test checks skill, so it can be used to offset the other token you picked. You have even reloaded your spell, so it lasts a little longer than [Shrivelling](/card/01060). _-_ ### ***WAIT, WHERES THE ENCOUNTER HANDLING SECTION???*** * **Your playing Diana, you dont need it.** _-_ #### *As always thanks for reading.
